opening: Dreams are the only place where memory edits itself in real time.

dreams.title: Cats in cages
dreams.excerpt: I ask whether the cats have finally gotten used to cages—the ones you need for the vet.
dreams.interpretation: Care appears as adaptation to constraint. A second strand treats music as an art form measured by whether youth will accept the listener.

dreams.title: Two named dogs
dreams.excerpt: Two dogs: [dog] small and skinny, [dog] larger—named, sized, kept.
dreams.interpretation: Companionship is itemized by body and name. The dream barely elaborates; naming is enough.

dreams.title: Florence reunion
dreams.excerpt: Study abroad in Florence again—except now everyone I have ever known is there.
dreams.interpretation: The dream collapses timelines so every peer becomes a witness. Success is staged as reunion; photographs that do not exist still demand to be proven.

dreams.title: Language among idols
dreams.excerpt: In class I ask [idol] a question; beside me [idol] asks how to write a character—and no language can finish the joke.
dreams.interpretation: Idols fill a classroom where literacy fails. The dream treats communication as a shared embarrassment: vocabulary cannot bridge who never learned the language of the room.

dreams.title: Watermelon neighbor
dreams.excerpt: A verge-old friend next door—ball, watermelon, stars—leaves and never comes back.
dreams.interpretation: A parallel-universe friendship ends in disappearance and grudge. Reunion at home is mediated by a mother’s joke about who liked whom.

dreams.title: Grandpa well again
dreams.excerpt: Grandpa is well again in cosplay with grandma—red suit, brown suit—I hug them both.
dreams.interpretation: Illness reverses into costume and comedy. A second strand seats the dreamer in a campus comedy club among parents and students.

dreams.title: BL mashup
dreams.excerpt: BL plot lines mash into tropes; the main figure keeps shifting roles.
dreams.interpretation: Story logic becomes a collage of romance tropes—the dream watches genre assemble itself.

dreams.title: Editor's song
dreams.excerpt: In a very serious situation you hear a song—and all the subtext is already inside it.
dreams.interpretation: Metadata appears as audible content: song information must live inside the music itself, collapsing documentation into performance.

dreams.title: Roommate packing
dreams.excerpt: Dorm door left open—I wake to my roommate packing luggage; she has no exams, I do.
dreams.interpretation: Asymmetry of obligation: one person leaves; the other remains inside the testing calendar.

dreams.title: California mountains
dreams.excerpt: Mountains so colorful I photograph cows and dogs—then learn I must ward off spirits in the mirror.
dreams.interpretation: Pastoral abundance flips into night duty. The mirror speaks with a voice that is not the dreamer’s; care for others’ nightmares becomes a private ordeal.

dreams.title: Fox and bingo rock
dreams.excerpt: A class fox, a numbered golden-green rock like bingo—then a teacher throws the rock away.
dreams.interpretation: Rules become accusation. Rain and a grandfather across the street restore recognition after public shame.

dreams.title: Animals close up
dreams.excerpt: Penguins break ice; rhinos hold peace signs—I tell mom looking at animals makes me happy.
dreams.interpretation: Joy is immediate and photographic. A parent’s prayer for grandparents’ recovery sits beside the dreamer’s private knowledge that they are not really getting better.

dreams.title: Twin daughters
dreams.excerpt: Twin girls with little bobs—mid-dream the logic of kinship collapses, then they are simply happy.
dreams.interpretation: The dream stages an impossible family structure, interrupts itself with ethical alarm, then returns to the children’s smiles as if the contradiction could be lived with.

dreams.title: Lotus incense plaza
dreams.excerpt: Artificial lotuses release incense; torso-shaped bags with past-year designs outshine this season’s crocodile shine.
dreams.interpretation: Shopping becomes a museum of surfaces. Taste is comparative—what was better last year haunts what is for sale now.

dreams.title: Parallel graduation
dreams.excerpt: Same schools, slightly different photos. A childhood voice arrives pitched higher than memory allows.
dreams.interpretation: Graduation recurs as a fork in continuity—images and pitch mark a self that is almost the same, almost happier, still accountable to a parent’s complaint.

dreams.title: Library aquarium
dreams.excerpt: A library that is also an aquarium, with a food court attached—and a worker unkind to everyone.
dreams.interpretation: Hybrid civic space stacks reading, display, and consumption into one building. Water and books share a roof; hostility appears at the counter.

dreams.title: Holiday drink
dreams.excerpt: A new drink I really want—mom says there are already so many at home; I say it’s the holidays.
dreams.interpretation: Desire negotiates with household surplus. The holiday licenses wanting one more thing.

dreams.title: Funeral practice rooms
dreams.excerpt: Office rooms where people practice the post-death process—living loved ones choose what others would eat at the funeral.
dreams.interpretation: Death is rehearsed as logistics while the loved one is still alive. Grief appears as menu selection in fluorescent rooms.

dreams.title: Mom at the concert
dreams.excerpt: I bring mom to a Seventeen concert; idols leave the stage—high-fives, Chinese hello, a tongue-twister line.
dreams.interpretation: Idol proximity and parental presence overlap. A performer recalls taking his own mother; the dream mirrors care across the barrier of the stage.

dreams.title: Grandpa young again
dreams.excerpt: Grandpa talks—he is in his twenties, remembers mom, does not remember me. I cried in the dream.
dreams.interpretation: Dementia is rewritten as youth with selective memory. Technology feels new to him; recognition skips a generation and lands as grief.

dreams.title: Pitch and gender
dreams.excerpt: Raising the key changes a song differently in a male voice than in a female one—words begin to distort.
dreams.interpretation: The dream rehearses a research task: compare gendered vocal effects under transposition, then prove it with four examples.

dreams.title: Nine-tailed fox drama
dreams.excerpt: A K-drama about religion—a nine-tailed fox gains a tail every fifty years—then breaks the fourth wall.
dreams.interpretation: The dreamer is both inside the episode and at a fan viewing. Belief systems accumulate like tails; the show discusses other shows about belief.

dreams.title: Paused park
dreams.excerpt: Everything in the amusement park was paused—literally stopped in the air.
dreams.interpretation: Process freezes mid-gesture. Talk of stocks and condos sits beside a nearly empty clubhouse where glare makes nearby things invisible.

dreams.title: Worn-out labels
dreams.excerpt: Sweatshirts labeled worn out—don’t wear. Rich people never have to donate; they can keep every piece.
dreams.interpretation: Clothing becomes an archive of class. Space itself is the privilege that lets nothing be discarded.

dreams.title: Concert interface
dreams.excerpt: Click the screen and dirt comes off; the interface listens for the concert’s vibrations.
dreams.interpretation: A live performance is rewritten as interactive UI. Attention splits between haptic design and a companion who appears only as a confused visual.

dreams.title: Temple of rounds
dreams.excerpt: At the last second everything reverses—then still explodes. Lights return to the temple seams for another round.
dreams.interpretation: Power cycles through sun-and-moon light in maze walls. Time reversal fails to prevent endings; identity resets as lights in the architecture.

dreams.title: Traumatic marriage
dreams.excerpt: A marriage that the note itself marks as politely traumatic.
dreams.interpretation: The dream is almost only a verdict. Partnership appears as a punchline that refuses elaboration.

patterns.label: Hybrid architecture
patterns.summary: Spaces refuse single function: library∩aquarium∩food court; temple∩maze; amusement park that is not Disneyland; funeral offices; shopping plazas as museums of surfaces.

patterns.label: Mediated sound
patterns.summary: Music and voice recur as systems—metadata inside songs, pitch distortion, pitch-shifted childhood speech, concert UIs, idol stages, music judged by youth acceptance.

patterns.label: Suspended or reversed time
patterns.summary: Parks freeze mid-air; temple combat reverses; illness reverses into wellness or youth; memory skips generations.

patterns.label: Parallel identity
patterns.summary: Slightly different graduations, gods who reset as lights, childhood friends in parallel universes, surnames performed for family on a bus.

patterns.label: Performance as interface
patterns.summary: Songs, concerts, dramas, and vocal keys become editable or enterable surfaces—clicked, annotated, high-fived, or broken through a fourth wall.

patterns.label: Kinship and care
patterns.summary: Parents, grandparents, and elders recur as recognition, prayer, complaint, concert companions, or figures who remember the wrong generation.

patterns.label: Animals as witness
patterns.summary: Dogs, foxes, penguins, cows, cats, and fox-spirits mark happiness, classroom games, pastoral wealth, veterinary cages, and mythic accumulation of time.

patterns.label: Class and space
patterns.summary: Wealth appears as closet space, paused condos, mountain houses with cows, and shopping plazas where last year’s design still wins.
